evals.prompt: How do I wrap errors in Go to preserve context?
evals.expected_output: Use fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) to wrap. Use errors.Is() and errors.As() to unwrap. Never just return err without adding context.

evals.prompt: How do I check if an error is of a specific type in Go?
evals.expected_output: Use errors.As() for error types (structs). Use errors.Is() for sentinel errors. Never compare error strings with err.Error() == "foo".

evals.prompt: When is it OK to use panic in Go?
evals.expected_output: Only for unrecoverable startup errors or programming invariants. Never panic in library code or as a substitute for error returns. Log and return errors in normal flows.

assertions.description: Limits panic to startup or invariants

should_not_trigger: How do I handle exceptions in Java?
How do I use try-catch in TypeScript?
How do I implement NestJS exception filters?
